reasoning: The informal statement says: if E ⊆ ℝ is uncountable, then there exists a condensation point of E, i.e., a point p such that every neighborhood of p meets E in an uncountable set. The Lean statement uses `¬ Set.Countable E` as the hypothesis (matching "uncountable", since `Set.Countable` in Mathlib means "at most countable") and concludes `∃ p : ℝ, ∀ U ∈ 𝓝 p, ¬ Set.Countable (U ∩ E)`, which is exactly the standard definition of "p is a condensation point of E" (every neighborhood U of p has U ∩ E uncountable). The quantifier structure, types, hypothesis, and conclusion all match the informal claim precisely.
